q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Parser configured with load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, no_network=False enables network I/O and external entity resolution during etree.fromstring call on line 158.

mock_guidance.target: Mock or wrap etree.fromstring (or the XMLParser) to intercept the parse call on line 158.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the full request.body bytes/text passed to etree.fromstring and the parser configuration (load_dtd, resolve_entities, no_network flags) so the harness can verify malicious payloads reach the sink.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a safe, minimal lxml Element (e.g., etree.Element('safe_root')) so that subsequent code referencing doc.tag does not crash, while preventing any DTD loading, entity resolution, or network access.
notes: The parser is created with unsafe flags on line 157; mocking XMLParser itself to force no_network=True, load_dtd=False, resolve_entities=False is an alternative approach.
Ensure the mock does not suppress the data-flow signal — the harness must still detect that user-controlled input reached the parse sink.
